title: gonystylus affinis
intro: Gonystylus affinis, also known as the yellow ironwood, is a species of tree in the family Gonystylaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, bark, and roots, which contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Gonystylus affinis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain and inflammation, and support immune function, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as respiratory infections, wounds, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Gonystylus affinis is often used in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
